projects.raw_text:
Smart Agriculture using sensors and ARDUINO.

Jun 2018 – Present

Project on smart agriculture using hardware devices like temperature sensor, humidity sensor, soil moisture sensor, ARDUINO microprocessor. The temperature and humidity of plant and moisture of soil was detected and their value was displayed on the mobile via Bluetooth. The code was built in ARDUINO software.

RTL to GDS Flow

Feb 2021 – May 2021

Guide: Dr. Sneh Saurabh
The RTL code written for a problem, logic synthesis, code coverage analysis, rtl design 
synthesis, power & area report analysis, STA, logical equivalence checking, Scan-chain 
insertion for DFT. Physical Design: Floor planning, placement, Clock Tree Synthesis, Global 
& Detailed Routing, Power analysis, final GDS.

Single Ended Sense Amplifier

Feb 2021 – May 2021

Guide: Dr. Anuj Grover 
Designed a Single Ended Sense Amplifier with symmetrical layout in cadence virtuoso 
Layout-XL with a charge sharing based reference circuit and achieved the specification of 
offset voltage of 60mV and SA-enable to Qlatch delay of 120ps at 1.08V worst case 
condition.

AOI222 Complex Gate Design (Domino)

Oct 2020 – Dec 2020

Guide: Dr. Anuj Grover
Design of a standard cell complex gate schematic and layout of domino AOI222 with 12T
height in cadence virtuoso 65nm technology. Comparison of pre-layout and post-layout
simulation results and the final power, performance and area analysis. 

Bank Retail Automation

Sep 2020 – Dec 2020

Guide: Naveen Prakash
Implemented a bank retail automation system in python using the principles of object
oriented programming. The system provided the customer the facility of opening various
types of accounts ranging from savings types to loan types.

Gain Enhancement Folded Cascode Amplifier

Oct 2020 – Dec 2020

Guide: Dr. G.S Visweswaran
Design of a Folded Cascode Amplifier using a novel positive feedback method with enhanced
gain without affecting the ugf, stability, power dissipation and output voltage swing of a
conventional FCA in a standard 0.18um TSMC 1.8V CMOS Technology in cadence virtuoso.

Scratchpad based memory interface between the accelerator and the host CPU

Oct 2020 – Dec 2020

Guide: Dr. Sujay Deb
Implementation of a scratchpad based memory interface shared between the accelerator
and the host CPU to improve the flexibility of hardware accelerators for neural networks
computation. Comparison of speed, area and power with the conventional DMA method.
